title: Bootstrap the example app scaffold, services, routing, DI, and mock data
blocks: Every phase-3 view-model task — they construct against these services.
Phase-4 view tasks — they build against view models provided through this scaffold.
description: Lay the foundation of the example app that sits under `turbo_widgets/example/`. This task produces the domain-free plumbing that every showcase view depends on: the folder structure, the routing enum, the list of all showcase entries, the mock content service, the theme service, the dependency-injection composition root, and the pubspec updates. It does NOT build the shell view, any showcase view, or any view model — those are later phases.

context.example_app_today: turbo_widgets/example currently contains only a throwaway main.dart and a constants folder. It needs to become a full Flutter app package named turbo_widgets_example that imports the restructured turbo_widgets library (phase 1 output).
context.fake_shop_metaphor: The example app is a fake shop called 'Turbo Widgets Shop' where every public widget from the package is a browsable 'product'. Opening a product opens a live preview page with debug sliders, dropdowns, and toggles that mutate the widget's inputs in real time. No cart, no checkout, no fake commerce — browsing and interacting with widgets only.
context.five_aisles: Navigation, Collections, Detail & Forms, Layout & Motion, Utilities. The product catalog must list every public widget under the correct aisle — the full inventory is spelled out in the spec under navigation_structure.aisles.
context.state_ownership_rule: Mutable state never lives inside views. Every screen's state sits on reactive cells (TNotifier<T>) exposed by a view model that extends TBaseViewModel from turbo_mvvm. This scaffold task sets up the services those view models will consume — not the view models themselves.

goals: example/pubspec.yaml declares explicit dependencies on turbo_widgets, turbo_mvvm, turbo_notifiers, turbo_forms, and shadcn_ui, all aligned with the versions used elsewhere in the monorepo.
example/lib/ has a clean domain-aligned folder tree matching the architecture.folder_layout section of the spec.
A ShowcaseRoute enum enumerates every route the app can display: shop_home, aisle_detail, and one product_detail variant per public widget.
A ShowcaseEntry model captures a single product's metadata: widget name, aisle key, tagline, route, and builder references.
A ShowcaseCatalogService lists every ShowcaseEntry that exists in turbo_widgets today, grouped by aisle, and supports lookup by route + by aisle.

A MockContentService produces neutral placeholder content the showcase views will render — sample names, descriptions, metadata rows, markdown snippets, calendar dates, plan cards — with zero invented business concepts.
A ThemeService owns a ValueListenable<Brightness> that the top app bar will toggle to flip light/dark mode.
A composition root (example/lib/core/globals/dependency_injection.dart) wires all of the above together and exposes them through constructor injection — no hidden globals from inside view models.
A ShowcaseRouter resolves a ShowcaseRoute to the corresponding view widget — but for this task, every route temporarily resolves to a simple placeholder until the real views land in later phases.

main.dart runs the shell; while the actual ShowcaseShellView is built by a later task, main.dart should compile and launch a minimal root widget (ShadApp + ContextualButtonsProvider + TResponsiveBuilder) that currently displays a placeholder.
non_goals: Building the ShowcaseShellView, ShowcaseShellViewModel, or any showcase view/view model — later phases own those.
Wiring the contextual buttons config per route — later phase after view models exist.
Implementing any real preview of a widget in the placeholder router output.
Writing the add-a-new-widget README playbook — that has its own task in the final phase.
Adding Firebase, analytics, networking, or any backend dependency.
acceptance_criteria: `flutter pub get` inside example/ succeeds with no version resolution warnings.
`flutter analyze` inside example/ reports zero findings.
`flutter run` inside example/ launches the app to a placeholder screen without crashing on a macOS/iOS/Android target of the developer's choice.
The ShowcaseRoute enum contains one value per public widget in turbo_widgets, verifiable by cross-referencing the aisle inventories in the spec.
ShowcaseCatalogService.byAisle('navigation') returns exactly the widget names listed under the navigation aisle in the spec. Same for every other aisle.
MockContentService exposes methods returning non-empty lists for categories, collection items, plans, metadata fields, and at least one markdown snippet.
ThemeService.brightness is a ValueListenable<Brightness> that flips between light and dark when a toggle method is called.

The composition root exposes a single function that builds the root widget tree fully wired with services passed as dependencies — no service look-ups hidden inside views or view models at this stage.
constraints: Follow dev-conventions/folders-and-file-types.md: one type per file, snake_case filenames, concept folders only, no arbitrary grouping layers.
Mock content must be generic placeholder content — no fake brand names, no invented commerce fiction. 'Sample Product A / B / C' is fine; 'Turbo Toaster' is not.
No business state inside views (none are authored in this task anyway).
No hardcoded mock data inside view models — view models (built later) will consume MockContentService via constructor injection.
Icons come exclusively from LucideIcons bundled with shadcn_ui. No new icon packages.
The scaffold must be ready for later tasks to plug view models in without rewiring the root.
tools_to_use: RepoPrompt MCP (file_search, get_file_tree, read_file) to inventory the current turbo_widgets exports.
dart-mcp (pub, analyze_files, dart_format, launch_app, list_devices) for pub get, analyze, and a smoke launch.
Bash for `flutter pub get`, `flutter analyze`, `flutter run`.

testing_and_verification.unit_tests: Write unit tests for ShowcaseCatalogService and MockContentService because both are pure business logic with deterministic outputs. Test: (1) catalog returns the correct aisle for a known widget name, (2) catalog byAisle returns the expected count and ordering for each aisle key, (3) catalog lookup by route returns a matching entry, (4) MockContentService returns non-empty, well-formed placeholder content. No mocks — these services have no external dependencies. Follow TestRulesAndReasons: Gherkin-style structure, no string-matching on error messages, focus on observable outcomes.
verification_gate: `flutter test` inside example/ passes the new unit tests.
`flutter run` launches to the placeholder screen without errors.

quality_checklist.maintainability: Services must be trivially discoverable from folder structure alone. Adding a new widget later should require touching the catalog service and one new showcase view folder — nothing else.
quality_checklist.dry: Every piece of mock data is owned by MockContentService. No duplication across services or widgets.
quality_checklist.single_responsibility: ShowcaseCatalogService only knows the product catalog. MockContentService only produces placeholder content. ThemeService only owns brightness state. Do not conflate.
quality_checklist.performance: Services return cached const lists where applicable — no repeated allocation on every call.
quality_checklist.abuse_resistance: Not applicable — example app has no user input validation surface at this stage.

worklog.summary: Bootstrapped the example app scaffold with domain-aligned folder structure, routing enum, catalog service, mock content service, theme service, LocatorService (GetIt-based composition root), and placeholder main.dart.
changes: Updated example/pubspec.yaml: added turbo_forms, turbolytics, get_it dependencies
Created core/enums/showcase_route.dart: enum with shopHome, aisleDetail, and 28 product detail routes
Created core/models/showcase_entry.dart: metadata model with widgetName, aisleKey, tagline, route, icon
Created core/services/showcase_catalog_service.dart: 28 entries across 5 aisles, byAisle/byRoute lookups, static aisle metadata
Created core/services/mock_content_service.dart: categories, collectionItems, plans, metadataFields, markdownContent
Created core/services/theme_service.dart: TNotifier<Brightness> with toggleBrightness(), ValueListenable exposure
Created core/routing/showcase_router.dart: resolves ShowcaseRoute to placeholder widgets
Created core/globals/locator_service.dart: LocatorService with Turbolytics mixin, registerInitialDependencies()
Rewrote main.dart: ShadApp + ContextualButtonsProvider<ShowcaseRoute> + TResponsiveBuilder + placeholder shell
Created test/core/services/showcase_catalog_service_test.dart: 14 tests covering entries, byAisle counts, byRoute lookups, aisle metadata
Created test/core/services/mock_content_service_test.dart: 9 tests covering all content accessors
Created test/core/services/theme_service_test.dart: 4 tests covering default, toggle, round-trip, and notification
verification: make analyze package=turbo_widgets: 0 issues (parent package unaffected)

workflow_instructions: Immediately set this task's `status` to `in-progress` after reading it.
Stay strictly inside example/. Do not touch the package source — phase 1 already reorganised it.
Run `flutter analyze` after every meaningful change. Do not batch and hope.
When done, set `status` to `done`, append a `worklog` array, then move the file to `workspace/tasks/archive/dev/02-bootstrap-example-scaffold-and-services-task.json`.
Scan other open tasks — add `follow_up_notes` entries to any task that would benefit from knowing the exact file paths of ShowcaseCatalogService, MockContentService, or ThemeService.
